title: Shape memory implants and a method and apparatus for the loading and implanting thereof
abstract: An orthopedic fixation system includes an implant and an implant insertion device. The implant transitions between a natural shape and an insertion shape. The implant includes a bridge defining a slot with a first end and a second end and first and second anchoring members extending from the bridge. The implant insertion device moves between a loaded position and an unloaded position. The implant insertion device in its loaded position inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant insertion device constrains the implant in its insertion shape. The implant insertion device in its unloaded position releases the implant.
claims:
Claims 1. An orthopedic fixation system, comprising: an implant transitionable between a natural shape and an insertion shape, the implant, comprising: a bridge defining a slot with a first end and a second end, and first and second anchoring members extending from the bridge; and an implant insertion device movable between a loaded position whereby the implant insertion device inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant insertion device constrains the implant in its insertion shape and an unloaded position whereby the implant insertion device releases the implant. 2. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 1, the implant insertion device comprising: a body including a first end and a second end; and an implant grip coupled with the body and movable relative to the body between an engaged position whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the implant grip releases the implant. 3. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 2, wherein the body defines a channel having an exit at the second end of the body further wherein the channel receives the implant grip such that the implant grip secures with the body whereby the implant grip in its engaged position extends from the channel and inserts through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape further whereby the implant grip in its disengaged position retracts into the channel to release the implant. 4. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 3, wherein the body defines a tamp at its second end whereby the tamp during loading of the implant insertion device with the implant abuts the bridge of the implant such that the exit for the channel of the body aligns with the slot of the bridge for the implant whereby extension of the implant grip from the channel inserts the implant grip through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape. 5. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 2, wherein: the body defines a channel having an exit at the second end of the body; and the implant grip, comprises: an actuator; and a paddle that inserts into the channel of the body, wherein the actuator connects with the paddle and secures the paddle with the body such that movement of the actuator relative to the body progresses the paddle between an engaged position whereby the paddle extends from the channel and inserts through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the paddle retracts into the channel to release the implant. 6. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 5, wherein the body defines a tamp at its second end whereby the tamp during loading of the implant insertion device with the implant abuts the bridge of the implant such that the exit for the channel of the body aligns with the slot of the bridge for the implant whereby extension of the paddle from the channel inserts the paddle through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape. 7. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 5, wherein the body defines a stop whereby, in progressing the paddle from its disengaged position to its engaged position, the actuator moves along the body towards the stop of the body until the actuator contacts the stop. 8. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 5, wherein: the channel communicates exterior to the body; and the paddle, comprises: a shaft with a first end and a second end whereby, when the paddle inserts into the channel of the body, the shaft at its first end is exposed exterior to the body such that the shaft is engageable with the actuator, and a blade coupled with the shaft at its second end, the blade including a leading edge between first and second sides, wherein movement of the actuator relative to the body progresses the paddle between an engaged position whereby the blade beginning at its leading edge extends from the channel and inserts through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge with its first and second sides abutting respective first and second ends of the slot thereby constraining the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the blade retracts into the channel to release the implant. 9. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 8, wherein the channel comprises a shaft channel configured to receive the shaft of the paddle therein and a blade channel configured to receive the blade of the paddle therein. 10. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 1, wherein the bridge comprises a first bridge segment and a second bridge segment that define the slot therebetween. 11. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 2, wherein the bridge includes a first transition section at a first end thereof and a second transition section at a second end thereof 

whereby the first and second transition sections deform to transition the implant between its natural shape and its insertion shape further whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second transition sections such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 12. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 11, wherein the first anchoring member extends from the first transition section of the bridge and the second anchoring member extends from the second transition section of the bridge whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the first and second anchoring members such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 13. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 2, wherein the bridge includes a transition section at a center section of the implant whereby the transition section deforms to transition the implant between its natural shape and its insertion shape further whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 14. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 13, wherein a first anchoring member extends from the bridge and a second anchoring member extends from the bridge whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the first and second anchoring members such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 15. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 14, wherein a third anchoring member exterior to the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof and a fourth anchoring member exterior to the second anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof. 16. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 14, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof; the second anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof; a third anchoring member including a passage extends from the bridge interior to the first anchoring member; a fourth anchoring member including a passage extends from the bridge interior to the second anchoring member; and the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and the passages of the third and fourth anchoring members whereby the implant grip engages the first and second anchoring members such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 17. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 13, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; the second anchoring member adjacent the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at the first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; and a third anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof adjacent the second end of the slot. 18. The orthopedic fixation system according to claim 13, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; the second anchoring member adjacent the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at the first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; and a third anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof offset from the 

second end of the slot; and a fourth anchoring member adjacent the third anchoring member extends from the bridge at the second end thereof offset from the second end of the slot. 19. An implant insertion device adapted for use with an implant transitionable between a natural shape and an insertion shape, wherein the implant insertion device is movable between a loaded position whereby the implant insertion device constrains the implant in its insertion shape and an unloaded position whereby the implant insertion device releases the implant, the implant insertion device, comprising: a body including a first end and a second end; and an implant grip coupled with the body and movable relative to the body between an engaged position whereby the implant grip is adapted to insert through a slot of a bridge for the implant and engage the bridge at first and second ends of the slot such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the implant grip is adapted to release the implant. 20. The implant insertion device according to claim 19, wherein the body defines a channel having an exit at the second end of the body further wherein the channel receives the implant grip such that the implant grip secures with the body whereby the implant grip in its engaged position is adapted to extend from the channel and insert through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape further whereby the implant grip in its disengaged position is adapted to retract into the channel to release the implant. 21. The implant insertion device according to claim 20, wherein the body defines a tamp at its second end whereby the tamp during loading of the implant insertion device with the implant is 

adapted to abut the bridge of the implant further whereby the exit for the channel of the body is adapted to align with the slot of the bridge for the implant such that extension of the implant grip from the channel inserts the implant grip through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape. 22. The implant insertion device according to claim 19, wherein: the body defines a channel having an exit at the second end of the body; and the implant grip, comprises: an actuator; and a paddle that inserts into the channel of the body, wherein the actuator connects with the paddle and secures the paddle with the body such that movement of the actuator relative to the body progresses the paddle between an engaged position whereby the paddle is adapted to extend from the channel and insert through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the paddle is adapted to retract into the channel to release the implant. 23. The implant insertion device according to claim 22, wherein the body defines a tamp at its second end whereby the tamp during loading of the implant insertion device with the implant is adapted to abut the bridge of the implant further whereby the exit for the channel of the body is adapted to align with the slot of the bridge for the implant such that extension of the paddle from the channel inserts the paddle through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape. 24. The implant insertion device according to claim 22, wherein the body defines a stop whereby, in progressing the paddle from its disengaged position to its engaged position, the 

actuator moves along the body towards the stop of the body until the actuator contacts the stop. 25. The implant insertion device according to claim 22, wherein: the channel communicates exterior to the body; and the paddle, comprises: a shaft with a first end and a second end whereby, when the paddle inserts into the channel of the body, the shaft at its first end is exposed exterior to the body such that the shaft is engageable with the actuator, and a blade coupled with the shaft at its second end, the blade including a leading edge between first and second sides, wherein movement of the actuator relative to the body progresses the paddle between an engaged position whereby the blade beginning at its leading edge is adapted to extend from the channel and insert through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge with its first and second sides abutting respective first and second ends of the slot thereby constraining the implant in its insertion shape and a disengaged position whereby the blade is adapted to retract into the channel to release the implant. 26. The implant insertion device according to claim 25, wherein the channel comprises a shaft channel configured to receive the shaft of the paddle therein and a blade channel configured to receive the blade of the paddle therein. 27. An implant transitionable between a natural shape and an insertion shape, the implant adapted for use with an implant insertion device movable between a loaded position whereby the implant insertion device constrains the implant in its insertion shape and an unloaded position whereby the implant insertion device releases the implant, the implant, comprising: a bridge defining a slot with a first end and a second end, whereby the slot is adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages the bridge at 

the first and second ends of the slot thereby constraining the implant in its insertion shape; and first and second anchoring members extending from the bridge. 28. The implant according to claim 27, wherein the bridge comprises a first bridge segment and a second bridge segment that define the slot therebetween. 29. The implant according to claim 27, wherein the bridge includes a first transition section at a first end thereof and a second transition section at a second end thereof whereby the first and second transition sections deform to transition the implant between its natural shape and its insertion shape further whereby the slot of the bridge is adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages the bridge at the first and second transition sections and constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 30. The implant according to claim 29, wherein the first anchoring member extends from the first transition section of the bridge and the second anchoring member extends from the second transition section of the bridge whereby the slot of the bridge is adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages the first and second anchoring members and constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 31. The implant according to claim 27, wherein the bridge includes a transition section at a center section of the implant whereby the transition section deforms to transition the implant between its natural shape and its insertion shape further whereby the slot of the bridge is adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 32. The implant according to claim 31, wherein the first anchoring member extends from the bridge and the second anchoring member extends from the bridge whereby the slot of the bridge is adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages 

the first and second anchoring members and constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 33. The implant according to claim 32, wherein a third anchoring member exterior to the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof and a fourth anchoring member exterior to the second anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof. 34. The implant according to claim 32, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof; the second anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof; a third anchoring member including a passage extends from the bridge interior to the first anchoring member; a fourth anchoring member including a passage extends from the bridge interior to the second anchoring member; and the slot of the bridge and the passages of the third and fourth anchoring members are adapted to receive the implant insertion device such that the implant insertion device engages the first and second anchoring members and constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 35. The implant according to claim 31, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at the first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; the second anchoring member adjacent the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at the first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; and a third anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof adjacent the second end of the slot. 36. The implant according to claim 31, wherein: the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at a first end thereof offset from the 

first end of the slot; the second anchoring member adjacent the first anchoring member extends from the bridge at the first end thereof offset from the first end of the slot; and a third anchoring member extends from the bridge at a second end thereof offset from the second end of the slot; and a fourth anchoring member adjacent the third anchoring member extends from the bridge at the second end thereof offset from the second end of the slot. 37. A method for an orthopedic fixation system, comprising: providing an implant transitionable between a natural shape and an insertion shape, the implant, comprising: a bridge defining a slot with a first end and a second end, and first and second anchoring members extending from the bridge; and loading an implant insertion device with the implant whereby the implant insertion device inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant insertion device constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 38. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 37, comprising: positioning the implant utilizing the implant insertion device with its first anchoring member at a first bone, its second anchoring member at a second bone, and its bridge spanning a fusion zone of the first and second bones; retracting the implant insertion device from the slot of the bridge while utilizing the implant insertion device to insert the first anchoring member into the first bone and the second anchoring member into the second bone until the bridge resides adjacent the first and second bones across the fusion zone thereof; and 

 removing the implant insertion device from the slot of the bridge whereby the implant insertion device releases the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant attempts to transition from its insertion shape to its natural shape. 39. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 37, wherein loading an implant insertion device with the implant, comprises: providing the implant insertion device, comprising: a body including a first end and a second end, and an implant grip coupled with the body; moving the implant grip relative to the body whereby the implant grip inserts through the slot of the bridge and engages the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant grip constrains the implant in its insertion shape. 40. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 39, comprising: positioning the implant utilizing the body of the implant insertion device with its first anchoring member at a first bone, its second anchoring member at a second bone, and its bridge spanning a fusion zone of the first and second bones; moving the implant grip relative to the body whereby the implant grip retracts from the slot of the bridge; inserting the first anchoring member into the first bone and the second anchoring member into the second bone utilizing the body until the bridge resides adjacent the first and second bones across the fusion zone thereof; and moving the implant grip relative to the body until the implant grip releases the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant attempts to transition from its insertion shape to its natural shape. 41. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 37, wherein loading an implant insertion device with the implant, comprises: providing the implant insertion device, comprising: a body including a first end and a second end, wherein the body defines a channel having an exit at the second end of the body, and an implant grip, comprising: an actuator, and a paddle that inserts into the channel of the body, wherein the actuator connects with the paddle and secures the paddle with the body; positioning the body relative to the implant whereby the second end of the body abuts the bridge of the implant and the exit from the channel aligns with the slot of the bridge; and moving the actuator relative to the body whereby the paddle extends from the channel and inserts through the slot of the bridge to engage the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot and constrain the implant in its insertion shape. 42. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 41, comprising: positioning the implant utilizing the body of the implant insertion device with its first anchoring member at a first bone, its second anchoring member at a second bone, and its bridge spanning a fusion zone of the first and second bones; moving the actuator relative to the body whereby the paddle retracts from the slot of the bridge into the channel of the body; inserting the first anchoring member into the first bone and the second anchoring member into the second bone utilizing the body until the bridge resides adjacent the first and second bones across the fusion zone thereof; and 

 moving the actuator relative to the body until the paddle releases the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant attempts to transition from its insertion shape to its natural shape. 43. The method for an orthopedic fixation system according to claim 41, comprising: positioning the implant utilizing the body of the implant insertion device with its first anchoring member at a first bone, its second anchoring member at a second bone, and its bridge spanning a fusion zone of the first and second bones; inserting the first anchoring member into the first bone and the second anchoring member into the second bone utilizing the body until the paddle contacts the first and second bones; pushing on the body whereby the second end of the body via the bridge inserts the first anchoring member into the first bone and the second anchoring member into the second bone further whereby the paddle via its contact with the first and second bones retracts from the slot of the bridge into the channel of the body until the bridge resides adjacent the first and second bones across the fusion zone thereof; and moving the actuator relative to the body until the paddle releases the bridge at the first and second ends of the slot such that the implant attempts to transition from its insertion shape to its natural shape.
